subject: Geography
title: New York City
description: A 60-second lesson on New York City.

hook.text: Manhattan was bought from the Lenape people in 1626. Today its real estate is worth trillions.

buildup.text: Its natural harbor made it America's gateway. Ellis Island welcomed 12 million immigrants.

discovery.text: The grid plan of 1811 organized future growth before most of Manhattan was even developed.

twist.text: Hurricane Sandy flooded subway tunnels in 2012, exposing the city's coastal vulnerability.

climax.text: New York proves that geography and ambition can build empires—and that nature always has the last word.

punchline.text: The city that never sleeps sits on an island that floods.

quiz.question: What geographic feature made New York City a major port?
